channel.title: Code Defence Intelligence Brief — Thu, 12 Mar 2026
channel.description: Daily cybersecurity intelligence and LinkedIn-ready executive posts for Thu, 12 Mar 2026.

item.title: Your workflow automation is now an active execution engine for attackers. ⚙️
item.cd:imagebrief: Dramatic dark-blue cinematic editorial. A digital gear icon fracturing with glowing red binary code leaking from the center. Deep navy and electric cyan palette. Bold white sans-serif overlay text: 'AUTOMATION EXPLOITED.' Code Defence logo bottom-right corner. 1200×628px.
item.description:
Your workflow automation is now an active execution engine for attackers. ⚙️
CVE-2025-68613 · Severity 9.9 · Critical Remote Code Execution in n8n.
The @[CISA](urn:li:organization:13010360) has added a critical expression injection flaw in n8n to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog today. This vulnerability allows an unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary system-level commands by exploiting the workflow expression evaluation system.
Current estimates show that over 24,000 n8n instances remain exposed on the internet without the necessary patches. Attackers are leveraging this to modify workflows, exfiltrate sensitive data from connected apps like Slack and Gmail, and gain full control over the underlying server environment.
The uncomfortable truth: Your automation platform has deeper access to your corporate data than almost any other tool, making it the highest-value target for initial entry.
→ Update n8n to version 1.120.4, 1.121.1, or 1.122.0 immediately. → Strictly isolate n8n instances from the public internet using a secure VPN or Zero Trust gateway. → Audit your workflow logs for any unauthorized modifications or anomalous outbound API requests.
Have you confirmed that your automation platform is not part of the 24,000 unpatched instances exposed online? 👇
#Cybersecurity #AppSec #Automation #ZeroTrust #CISO #CodeDefence

item.title: A geopolitical wiper attack has crippled a global medical technology giant. 🛑
item.cd:imagebrief: Cinematic editorial style. A digital medical monitor showing static and a 'Handala' logo overlay. Deep navy and electric cyan palette. Bold white sans-serif overlay text: 'WIPER ATTACK DISRUPTION.' Code Defence logo bottom-right corner. 1200×628px.
item.description:
A geopolitical wiper attack has crippled a global medical technology giant. 🛑
Disruption Alert · Iran-linked 'Handala' group claims wiper attack on Stryker.
In the last 24 hours, @[Stryker](urn:li:organization:1592) has faced a massive systems outage following a destructive wiper attack. The pro-Palestinian group Handala has claimed responsibility, stating they have wiped thousands of @[Microsoft](urn:li:organization:1035) Windows endpoints across the company's global network in retaliation for geopolitical events.
This attack represents a significant escalation, shifting from financially motivated ransomware to purely destructive operations targeting critical healthcare supply chains. Reports indicate that corporate laptops and servers were suddenly disabled, with the attackers' logo appearing on encrypted or wiped screens.
The uncomfortable truth: Your disaster recovery plan is no longer just for hardware failure; it is now a frontline defense against geopolitical cyber warfare.
→ Ensure your offline backups are current and have been tested for full-scale restoration. → Implement strict network segmentation to prevent wiper malware from moving laterally across the globe. → Monitor for unusual 'cleaning' or disk-level utility execution across your Windows fleet.
Is your incident response plan prepared for a scenario where your entire endpoint fleet is suddenly unbootable? 👇
#Cybersecurity #HealthcareSecurity #WiperMalware #IncidentResponse #SOC #CodeDefence

item.title: Your older Apple devices are now targets for the Coruna exploit kit. 📱
item.cd:imagebrief: Graphic design poster style. A rusted digital chain with a glowing red link representing a WebKit exploit. Charcoal grey and warning yellow palette. Bold white sans-serif overlay text: 'LEGACY BROWSER RISK.' Code Defence logo bottom-right corner. 1200×628px.
item.description:
Your older Apple devices are now targets for the Coruna exploit kit. 📱
CVE-2023-43010 · Severity 8.8 · Active WebKit Exploitation in Legacy iOS and macOS.
@[Apple](urn:li:organization:162479) has backported security fixes to older versions of iOS, iPadOS, and macOS Sonoma today. This follows the discovery of active exploitation by the Coruna exploit kit, which leverages a WebKit memory corruption flaw to achieve remote code execution.
The Coruna kit is a sophisticated toolkit used in cyberespionage and crypto-theft campaigns. By targeting the browser engine, attackers can compromise a device simply when a user views a maliciously crafted web page. This highlights the extreme risk of maintaining legacy unpatched devices within an enterprise environment.
The uncomfortable truth: Your security posture is only as strong as the oldest, unpatched iPad in your executive's home office.
→ Force a security update for all Apple devices, including older hardware, to the latest backported releases today. → Use MDM to identify and isolate any devices that are no longer receiving security updates from Apple. → Enable Lockdown Mode for high-value targets to reduce the attack surface for complex WebKit exploits.
Are you still allowing legacy, end-of-life mobile devices to connect to your corporate email? 👇
#Cybersecurity #MobileSecurity #Apple #ThreatIntelligence #SOC #CodeDefence

item.title: One unauthenticated request can now compromise your SQL Server environment. 🐚
item.cd:imagebrief: Data-viz style. A digital database icon with a bright red 'Sysadmin' badge being unlocked. Matte black and deep gold palette. Bold white sans-serif overlay text: 'DATABASE ACCESS EXPOSED.' Code Defence logo bottom-right corner. 1200×628px.
item.description:
One unauthenticated request can now compromise your SQL Server environment. 🐚
CVE-2026-21262 · Severity 8.8 · Active Privilege Escalation in Microsoft SQL Server.
Following the March Patch Tuesday release, we are seeing a spike in interest and scanning activity for CVE-2026-21262. This publicly disclosed flaw allows an authorized attacker with low-level access to elevate their privileges to 'sysadmin' over the network.
This vulnerability is particularly dangerous because it bypasses the standard identity boundaries within the database environment. If an attacker has gained a foothold anywhere in your network, this flaw provides a direct path to the crown jewels—your corporate data.
The uncomfortable truth: Your database encryption and access controls mean nothing if an attacker can simply promote themselves to a system administrator.
→ Apply the March 2026 @[Microsoft](urn:li:organization:1035) security updates to all SQL Server instances immediately. → Enforce the principle of least privilege and conduct a review of all accounts with network access to SQL instances. → Monitor for unusual administrative account promotions or schema changes within your databases.
Is your database patching cycle as aggressive as your workstation update policy? 👇
#Cybersecurity #DatabaseSecurity #SQLServer #VulnerabilityManagement #SOC #CodeDefence
